title: exotic xanthan-infused Turkish mushroom Menemen
description: pungent, chewy Turkish dish featuring mushrooms, perfect for a date night. This recipe is inspired by traditional Turkish cuisine and adapted to include xanthan-infused mushrooms for added depth.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in xanthan gum, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Create a small well in the center of the mushroom mixture and pour in beaten eggs.
Cook until eggs are set, about 2-3 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare lotus leaves according to package instructions.
Spoon mushroom and egg mixture onto center of each leaf, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold leaves over filling to form a neat package.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional xanthan-infused mushrooms if desired.
To make xanthan-infused mushrooms, slice mushrooms and marinate in a mixture of 1 tsp xanthan gum, 1/4 cup olive oil, and 1/4 cup lemon juice for at least 30 minutes.
Bake xanthan-infused mushrooms for 10-15 minutes, or until tender.
